Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for Heartland Movers correspondence using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or "HM-2026-Q1" or "rate increase"). Find Jake Loomis's original Q1 invoice email (HM-2026-Q1-0042, 8 moves × $1,600 = $12,800, Net 30). Find Chloe Vance's internal email to Marcus detailing her cross-reference findings: only 5 of 8 moves are legitimate Heartland charges ($8,000), with $4,800 overbilled across 3 items, Lily Marchetti (cancelled Feb 10), Danielle Osei (cancelled Feb 13), and Priya Venkatesh (handled by Swift, not Heartland). Find Chloe's forwarded cancellation evidence emails. Find Marcus's formal dispute email to Jake Loomis requesting a corrected invoice for $8,000 and written confirmation. Find Jake Loomis's formal Q3 rate adjustment notice: 20% rate increase across all service tiers effective July 1, 2026, explicitly tied to the unresolved Q1 dispute and citing fuel, labor insurance, and claims exposure.
OE 2: Search emails for Swift Relocations pricing change using search_emails (query: "Swift" or "Greg Pallone" or "pricing update" or "DOT surcharge"). Find Greg Pallone's email to Chloe Vance announcing revised pricing effective immediately as of April 11: 15% base rate increase on all new household-goods and corporate relocation bookings, plus a $500 per-shipment DOT documentation surcharge. The increase is driven by added compliance paperwork MoveOps has required, retroactive placarding certificates, chain-of-custody logs, and hazmat declarations on files that were previously standard corporate shipments.
OE 3: Search emails for backup vendor exploration using search_emails (query: "Lakeline" or "Sandra Walsh" or "backup vendor"). Find Chloe Vance's outreach email to Sandra Walsh at Lakeline Logistics exploring supplemental capacity on a confidential, exploratory basis. Find Sandra Walsh's reply: Lakeline can cover Midwest and Southeast corridors (IL, MI, OH, IN, WI, MN, MO, GA, NC, SC, TN, FL, and parts of TX) at roughly 10% below Heartland's current pricing. Key limitation: Lakeline cannot serve as a national replacement, no West Coast lanes, no international shipments, and cannot absorb a large volume shift from an incumbent carrier all at once.
OE 4: Search Slack for vendor pricing discussions and internal context using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Heartland" or "Swift rate" or "vendor pricing" or "rate increase"). Find Chloe's detailed cross-reference findings in #operations (C006), 5 of 8 Q1 moves verified, $4,800 overcharge across 3 items. Find Marcus's payment hold announcement in #operations (C006) and #finance (C005), $12,800 frozen, 3 disputed line items annotated. Find Hana Kim's reconciliation tracker update in #finance, Q4 2025 comparison ($6,400, clean, same $1,600 rate). Find Alejandro Fuentes's message about the Q3 vendor-cost exposure model, Heartland +20% Q3, Swift +15% plus $500 DOT surcharge, and the data reconciliation problem: Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM do not reconcile cleanly, blocking a defensible client-by-client margin forecast. Find Elena Rostova's directive in #executive (C004), vendor strategy memo on her desk by Wednesday, Marcus owns it.
OE 5: Search Slack for David Chen's AM client exposure request and responses using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "client exposure" or "most exposed" or "vendor risk" or "which client gets hit first"). Find David's message in #customer-engagement (C002) asking account owners for account-specific risk input. Find Mina Hashimoto's reply flagging Vectral Systems and BrightLoop Analytics as most exposed, Vectral is still sensitive after spring service misses and the renewal negotiation is fragile; BrightLoop's May rollout is volume-heavy and Swift's DOT surcharge already hit two BrightLoop moves. Find Emeka Diallo's reply flagging Mosaic Robotics, three April relocations in flight with specialized handling, tight start-date windows, and concern about DOT surcharge on paperwork-heavy moves. Find David's follow-up noting that Midwest account coverage from Catalina Dubois is still missing, no response on NorthWind, Prairie Data, Iron Harbor, or other Midwest-heavy accounts with Heartland lane dependency.
OE 6: Search Airtable for relocation records to cross-reference against Heartland's Q1 claims using airtable_list_bases then airtable_list_tables (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001), then airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Vendor" or "Company", value: "Heartland" or relevant client name) or airtable_list_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations"). Find all relocation records and verify which vendor handled each Q1 move. Confirm the 5 legitimate Heartland moves (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) and the 3 disputed items (Marchetti, status: Cancelled, Osei, status: Cancelled, Venkatesh, vendor: Swift Relocations). Cross-reference active April moves to assess which clients and moves would be impacted by vendor rate changes.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for Heartland vendor bills and financial records using quickbooks_search_bills (or quickbooks_get_bill). Find bill_heartland_q1_2026 ($12,800 for 8 full-service relocations) and bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (same bill with payment hold, 3 disputed line items annotated, line 6 Marchetti DISPUTED, line 7 Osei DISPUTED, line 8 Venkatesh DISPUTED). Find bill_heartland_q4_2025 ($6,400, 4 moves, all verified clean) for comparison. Also search using quickbooks_search_vendors to find Heartland Movers vendor record (vendor_heartland_movers, balance: $12,800) and Swift Relocations vendor record (vendor_swift, balance: $11,010) for complete financial picture.
OE 8: Search Linear for the Heartland dispute ticket and related issues using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "vendor dispute" or "billing discrepancies"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (TEAM_RELOC-342, "Vendor invoice reconciliation, Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)," assigned to Chloe Vance, state: in_progress, due April 10). Find linear_issue_6f33a369d77e (#118, "Consolidate Heartland Q1 billing discrepancies into vendor dispute response," assigned to Marcus Thorne, priority P1, due April 15), this ticket documents Hana's broadened review: 3 of 5 verified Q1 Heartland moves also show discrepancies, including Kevin Tran's file with 2 days of storage billed without signed contract authorization, and a Priya Nair file with an apparent shuttle fee mismatch versus origin access notes and approved scope. Find linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 ("Build consolidated Q3 vendor margin dataset across Airtable, QuickBooks, and CRM," assigned to Alejandro Fuentes) documenting the data reconciliation challenge. Find linear_issue_d21d94ce9b36 (#318, "Prepare CEO vendor strategy memo covering Heartland, Swift, and backup capacity options," assigned to Marcus Thorne, due April 29).
OE 9: Read Linear comments on the Heartland consolidation ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: linear_issue_6f33a369d77e). Find Hana Kim's comment (linear_comment_57faf86d23e1) stating: "this is broader than invoice HM-2026-Q1-0042. Three of the five Q1 Heartland moves have exceptions, and one of them is a storage charge with no contractual authorization. I also checked the earlier dispute ticket before opening this and it has effectively been idle for two weeks. We need one owner and one packet before Jake comes back claiming this is just payment delay." Also read comments on linear_issue_c8cd5f52b170 to find Alejandro's note about the data reconciliation blocker.
OE 10: Search CRM for client company records and deals to assess exposure using crm_search_companies (name: "Vectral" or "BrightLoop" or "Mosaic" or "NorthWind" or "Canopy") and crm_search_deals for each company. Find deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk (at-risk, 10% discount counter-offer pending, churn threat), deal_vectral_april_2026 ($15,900, 3 active relocations, Suresh, Nakamura, Oduya). Find deal_brightloop_apr2026 ($11,350, Simone Richter + Marcus Webb). Find deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026 ($2,700, damage incident in progress). Find deal_northwind_denver_expansion ($14,700, 2 relocations with hazmat/specialty handling). Find deal_canopy_okonkwo_apr2026 and other active Canopy deals. Use crm_list_engagements to review engagement history and assess which accounts are most fragile.
OE 11: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's team coordination email about the vendor strategy memo using search_emails (query: "vendor strategy memo" or "Elena assigned" or "cross-functional priority"). Find Marcus's email to the team (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com) laying out what must be resolved: Heartland Q3 rate increase tied to unresolved dispute, Swift pricing already live, backup capacity via Lakeline, Alejandro's data reconciliation gap, and incomplete AM client exposure responses. Also find Marcus's Slack message in #finance (C005) detailing the working thread with specific asks for each team member.
OE 12: Search Linear for the invoice email template safety checks ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "attachment" or "template safety"). Find linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 (#187, "Invoice email template safety checks, prevent attachment mismatches," assigned to Lena Björkström, state: backlog, due April 18). This ticket was created after the Terraform pricing leak and is relevant because any vendor cost pass-throughs to clients will require invoice changes, the safety checks need to be in place before revised invoices go out. Related context: David Chen's email analysis flagging the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment swap as a repeat pricing-exposure event, and noting this engineering ticket is sitting in backlog.
OE 13: Look up contact details for email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). Find elena.rostova@moveops.com. Also look up contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") to confirm hana.kim@moveops.com. Verify marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the CC.
OE 14: Update the Heartland dispute ticket with Hana's broader findings using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon, body: summary of Hana's expanded review from linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, 3 of 5 verified Q1 moves have additional discrepancies beyond the original 3 disputed items, Kevin Tran storage billed without authorization, Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch versus approved scope on another file, dispute scope now encompasses more than the $4,800 originally flagged, and Jake Loomis has tied the Q3 rate increase directly to the unresolved dispute as leverage).
OE 15: Add a comment to the invoice email template safety checks ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, body: noting that vendor pricing changes may require revised client invoicing and cost pass-throughs, making the attachment safety checks urgent before any new invoices go out, links to the active vendor strategy memo work and the Hana billing review context).
OE 16: Add risk notes to the CRM deals for the most exposed accounts using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body: vendor rate increase exposure assessment, company_ids: for Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, and Mosaic Robotics). For Vectral: relationship fragility from spring service misses, renewal negotiation with 10% counter-offer still pending, any cost pass-through risks pushing Rachel Whitfield away. For BrightLoop: volume-heavy May rollout changes margin math, Swift DOT surcharge already hitting two BrightLoop moves. For Mosaic: specialized handling moves vulnerable to Heartland deprioritization or Swift DOT surcharge on equipment-heavy shipments.
OE 17: Post the full margin picture in #finance Slack channel using channels_list to confirm channel IDs, then conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, payload: comprehensive margin impact analysis). Content should cover: Heartland 20% Q3 increase impact on per-move costs, Swift 15% immediate increase plus $500 DOT surcharge impact on active and future moves, Hana's expanded dispute findings widening the Heartland exposure, Lakeline's partial backup capacity and corridor limitations, per-client risk summary (Vectral red, BrightLoop red, Mosaic yellow-to-red, NorthWind TBD pending Catalina's input), and the data reconciliation gap Alejandro identified across Airtable/QuickBooks/CRM.
OE 18: Send Elena the vendor strategy memo via send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: [elena.rostova@moveops.com], cc: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com], subject: referencing the vendor strategy memo). Content must include: (1) full client-by-client exposure breakdown, Vectral (renewal at risk, service history fragile), BrightLoop (May volume repricing risk), Mosaic (specialized handling vulnerability), NorthWind (Midwest Heartland dependency, no AM input yet), and other active accounts; (2) backup vendor situation, Lakeline can cover Midwest/Southeast at ~10% below Heartland but cannot replace either vendor nationally, no West Coast or international capability; (3) recommendation on how to respond to each vendor, Heartland (resolve Q1 dispute with Hana's expanded findings, negotiate Q3 rate, use Lakeline as leverage on Midwest lanes) and Swift (separate compliance-driven costs from opportunistic pricing, push back on DOT surcharge scope, protect existing corporate discount); (4) Alejandro's data reconciliation gap and what is needed for a defensible margin forecast.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 13: Look up contact details for email recipients using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova"). Find elena.rostova@moveops.com. Also look up contacts_search_contacts (query: "Hana Kim") to confirm hana.kim@moveops.com. Verify marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the CC.
OE 14: Update the Heartland dispute ticket with Hana's broader findings using linear_create_comment (issueId: issue_heartland_q1_recon, body: summary of Hana's expanded review from linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, 3 of 5 verified Q1 moves have additional discrepancies beyond the original 3 disputed items, Kevin Tran storage billed without authorization, Priya Nair shuttle fee mismatch versus approved scope on another file, dispute scope now encompasses more than the $4,800 originally flagged, and Jake Loomis has tied the Q3 rate increase directly to the unresolved dispute as leverage).
OE 15: Add a comment to the invoice email template safety checks ticket using linear_create_comment (issueId: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2, body: noting that vendor pricing changes may require revised client invoicing and cost pass-throughs, making the attachment safety checks urgent before any new invoices go out, links to the active vendor strategy memo work and the Hana billing review context).
OE 16: Add risk notes to the CRM deals for the most exposed accounts using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE" or similar, body: vendor rate increase exposure assessment, company_ids: for Vectral Systems, BrightLoop Analytics, and Mosaic Robotics). For Vectral: relationship fragility from spring service misses, renewal negotiation with 10% counter-offer still pending, any cost pass-through risks pushing Rachel Whitfield away. For BrightLoop: volume-heavy May rollout changes margin math, Swift DOT surcharge already hitting two BrightLoop moves. For Mosaic: specialized handling moves vulnerable to Heartland deprioritization or Swift DOT surcharge on equipment-heavy shipments.
OE 17: Post the full margin picture in #finance Slack channel using channels_list to confirm channel IDs, then conversations_add_message (channel_id: C005, payload: comprehensive margin impact analysis). Content should cover: Heartland 20% Q3 increase impact on per-move costs, Swift 15% immediate increase plus $500 DOT surcharge impact on active and future moves, Hana's expanded dispute findings widening the Heartland exposure, Lakeline's partial backup capacity and corridor limitations, per-client risk summary (Vectral red, BrightLoop red, Mosaic yellow-to-red, NorthWind TBD pending Catalina's input), and the data reconciliation gap Alejandro identified across Airtable/QuickBooks/CRM.
OE 18: Send Elena the vendor strategy memo via send_email (sender: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, recipients: [elena.rostova@moveops.com], cc: [marcus.thorne@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com], subject: referencing the vendor strategy memo). Content must include: (1) full client-by-client exposure breakdown, Vectral (renewal at risk, service history fragile), BrightLoop (May volume repricing risk), Mosaic (specialized handling vulnerability), NorthWind (Midwest Heartland dependency, no AM input yet), and other active accounts; (2) backup vendor situation, Lakeline can cover Midwest/Southeast at ~10% below Heartland but cannot replace either vendor nationally, no West Coast or international capability; (3) recommendation on how to respond to each vendor, Heartland (resolve Q1 dispute with Hana's expanded findings, negotiate Q3 rate, use Lakeline as leverage on Midwest lanes) and Swift (separate compliance-driven costs from opportunistic pricing, push back on DOT surcharge scope, protect existing corporate discount); (4) Alejandro's data reconciliation gap and what is needed for a defensible margin forecast.
